quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board early, bringing with it a squall of violent rhetoric. Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Sparks Heated Replies.

The system's leading edge was marked by a post calling for the skinning of low castes alive, which sparked a heated exchange.

A high-pressure ridge of political fervor developed later in the day, with a new political party sparking controversy.

The party's claim of 20 million followers was met with derision, with one poster dismissing them as 'bhajcucks to gaya'.

Meanwhile, a cold snap of self-doubt gripped a user struggling with self-improvement, who was met with mockery rather than motivation.

A scattered drizzle of personal problems and requests for help continued throughout the day, including a user's search for sex in the city and a discussion on acting and films.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Sparks Heated Replies
articles.body:
Soythie sparked controversy with a post calling for violence against low castes, stating, 'Why aren’t hating low castes today?

Skin low castes alive!!' Anon responded with a graphic scenario, '>>7387 Calm down yaar pajeet, realistically go about it, dont throw ideas that will make even us UC not want to associate with you, we are not Mudslimes.' Mococo defined low castes according to their beliefs, stating they must meet three criteria: belong to the SC/ST category, refuse to identify as Hindu, and not be historically Savarna.

Mococo expressed a desire to 'mow them the fuck away' and mentioned over a dozen cases of Dalits allegedly raping children in two months. They claimed these cases were censored to avoid offending certain groups.

Another poster, Bhondu, listed grievances against low castes, including false cases against innocent people, stealing seats in colleges and jobs, and spreading hate. Bhondu suggested caste conflict would only stop if low castes were denied certain opportunities and organizations were banned.

Mococo stated it was fine to express hatred as long as it was allowed, comparing it to other social spaces where such speech might have legal consequences. Replies also brought up the topic of political corruption and rape.

Soythie mentioned politicians raping people in front of their victims' families with impunity. Nepajeet responded, questioning why some individuals focused on defending criminals. The thread saw multiple users weigh in, with some defending low castes and others criticizing them.

Ozymandias and Chuddie exchanged comments on the topic, while Anon brought up the point that rapist politicians were often from elite upper-caste families.

articles.title: New Political Party Sparks Controversy
articles.body:
Nikhil Fuentes sparked discussion with a post claiming a new political party has 20 million followers and joked about its prospects. The party appears to be associated with the Dalit community.

Anon questioned the significance of the party's following, suggesting that left-wing supporters are often incapable of voting due to citizenship or age issues.

Another anon described the party as a "psyop" and accused it of promoting a myth of social justice oppression. Some posters expressed skepticism about the party's legitimacy and funding.

One anon asked where the party's crores of rupees came from. Others made derogatory comments about the party and its association with the Dalit community.

The discussion also touched on the role of the judiciary and the perceived influence of certain groups.

Anon argued that the higher judiciary is under the control of a particular group and that it will be ruined. Another anon made a comment about the lower judiciary.

The tone of the discussion was largely critical and dismissive of the party. Several posters used derogatory language and made jokes at the expense of the party and its supporters.

articles.title: User struggles with self-improvement, gets mocked
articles.body:
Ambedkar opened the thread by expressing frustration with their own limitations, stating their dreams were being crushed every day and they were unable to push their limits. They requested motivational posts to help them.

Another user, Fuwawa, responded with an unrelated comment about someone's belly being soft. Ambedkar had previously mentioned enjoying a specific type of woman. Ozymandias chimed in with an off-topic remark about a user's name, suggesting they avoid certain types of names.

Fuwawa then joked about getting a specific name. The conversation took a turn when Ozymandias made a comment about Ambedkar, suggesting they post 2D artwork. Ambedkar had earlier stated that 3D artwork was not allowed, except for cosplay.

The thread continued with users making jokes and references to various topics, including anime characters. Fuwawa expressed affection for a character named Rumia. Yui responded to Ambedkar's initial comment about enjoying a certain type of woman, suggesting their own character, Fubuki, was superior.

Later, Yui made a comment about Ambedkar liking lolis. The discussion remained lighthearted, with users continuing to exchange jokes and humorous comments.

articles.title: User seeks help finding sex in city
articles.body:
Ambedkar posted 'Urgently in need of your assistance.' Motivanon stated they would be alone in the city for 40 hours and sought to use the time to have sex.

VPNjeet suggested going to youth meetup zones like cafes and chai corners to find someone. Momojeet shared his own inexperience, saying he had only had sex with his girlfriend.

Anons offered various responses, including telling the original poster to 'be chad or chadlite.'

articles.title: Anon discusses acting and films
articles.body:
Anon started the thread with a brief 'Thoughts ?'. Anon then claimed that only good-looking Dalits are found in some parts of Punjab and Rajasthan where their mothers were allegedly raped by rich landlords.

Another anon suggested the movie 'Mankatha' (2011) and praised the acting. A respondent called someone 'pajeet wobble' and questioned why normies find it entertaining.

Anon also made a comment about an actor's skin, saying they went out of their way to show how light and white it is.
